body_txt: In a classic cartoon, any non-fatal (which is to say, almost every) head injury immediately results in a massive swelling the size of an orange or larger, usually pushing its pink, fleshy way through the victim's hair. Sometimes they come pre-bandaged . May also erupt in the exact shape of the object that caused them. Attempts to shove the lumps back into one's head will usually be answered with a lump of equal size appearing on the other side of the head... or sometimes somewhere else entirely. Occasionally, further damage will make the lump grow into an obelisk-shaped mountain or sprout another smaller lump. This can happen multiple times in a row, causing a very tall bump or a little cactus-like appearance. The spherical one is more popular in Anime and is sometimes used in conjunction with Instant Bandages to denote an enemy's weak spot . In contrast, the mound-shaped one is more popular in Western Animation , though both have appeared in either medium. While exaggerated , this trope is very much in Truth in Television ; even a minor bump to the head usually results in swelling that takes a while to go away, or remains permanent. However, in real life, the size of the bump wouldn't come close to what it would be on television; at its largest, a bump on the head may be roughly the size of a lime. May overlap with Post-Violence Warning . Sister-trope to Cartoon Throbbing . Not to be confused with Your Head A-Splode . Also, no relation to My Brain Is Big .
